title: ProDec 4' - 8' (1.2-2.4m) Button Lock Fibreglass & Aluminium Extension Pole for Paint Roller Frames,Gives Extended Reach Compatible with Screw & Push Fit Frames,4 feet - 8 feet Trade Professional Pole
features: Trade quality fibreglass and aluminium extension pole for paint roller frames
Easy adjustment to a range of lengths between 4' and 8' (120-240cm)
Button lock mechanism securely locks the pole in place when extended
Compatible with most paint roller frames via universal screw fitting or removable push fit adaptor
MORE INFORMATION BELOW in the 'From The Manufacturer' section - click on 'See More Product Details' or scroll down
description: From the manufacturer ProDec is the UK's biggest and most widely distributed brand of trade decorating accessories, so whether you're a seasoned pro, enthusiastic DIYer or even a beginner, we've got everything you need for a great finish - just add paint Power up your painting Power through painting jobs with this trade quality fibreglass and aluminium extension pole. The button lock mechanism adjusts to a range of lengths and securely locks the pole in place, making it ideal for painting ceilings and walls without ladders or platforms. It fits virtually all trade cage and double arm roller frames as well as DIY frames, having a push fit plastic adaptor that removes to reveal a universal steel screw fitting. Button lock mechanism The simple yet effective push-button mechanism locks securely into place on notches on the aluminium inner tube. Multi-position adjust Regular locking notches on the inner tube enables you to adjust the pole to a range of lengths to suit the job at hand. Heavy duty fibreglass body Pound for pound, fibreglass has significantly higher mechanical strength than steel, making it the ideal material for extension poles as it can be both lightweight and strong, so it won't let you down when the going gets tough. Screw and push fittings Compatible with virtually all cage and double arm roller frames: push fit frames attach via the plastic adaptor, which removes to reveal a universal steel fitting for attaching screw fit frames. PRODEC EXTENSION POLES - from heavy to light duty use, from smooth interior walls to rough masonry surfaces, ProDec has a range a extension poles designed to cope with the job Super Lock Elite ProDec's top of the range Super Lock pole features reinforcements in every department for power painting where you need it most. Ideal for emulsion painting and tough masonry jobs on textured surfaces. Button lock ProDec's best-selling button lock extension pole features rugged fibreglass & aluminium construction. Much stronger than metal-only poles, it's ideal for trade and DIY projects on both interior and exterior surfaces. Twist lock Featuring easy adjustment to any intermediate length via a rugged twist lock mechanism, this pole is ideal for DIY and light trade use on interior surfaces. Lightweight twist lock Ideal for light duty interior projects with rollers up to 9", this lightweight all-steel pole features a simple twist-to-lock that adjusts to any intermediate length. PILE DEPTH GUIDE - Selecting the right pile of roller for emulsion or masonry paint is critical to ensuring good coverage and a high quality finish. Use this guide to help you make the right choice. Short pile Suitable for smooth and semi-smooth surfaces such as plaster, drywall and ceilings. Because the pile is shorter it puts down less paint and gives you the smoothest of finishes. It's good if you're painting a similar colour over the previous one, but if the colour change is more significant you should consider choosing a medium pile as it will put down more paint and cover better. Short pile rollers can also be used with gloss and satin paints on smooth planed wood and metal. Medium pile The most popular pile used with emulsion paints and suitable for smooth and semi-smooth surfaces such as plaster, drywall and ceilings (including light to medium textured plasters such as artex). It is suitable for emulsion colour changes both similar to the previous one and more substantial such as light to dark or vice-versa. Depending on the fabric type, it can also be used on semi-smooth masonry surfaces such as brick. Long pile Suitable for semi-rough and rough surfaces such as brick, breeze block, cement and render, where the longer pile is effective at getting into the surface texture, as well as interior walls and ceilings (including medium to heavily textured plasters such as artex). As it lays down plenty of paint it is suitable for colour changes both similar to the previous one and more substantial such as light to dark or vice-versa. Can be used on smooth surfaces but won't give as good a finish as shorter pile rollers. Extra long pile Suitable for rough and very rough masonry surfaces such as brick, breeze block, cement, heavily textured renders, stucco (exterior cement plaster) and pebbledash. It gives maximum paint loading - essential for efficiency when painting masonry so you're reloading less often - and is effective at getting into surface textures for rapid application without missing areas.
